post_content: ACMI & Charter Availability – DC9 Fleet. We are pleased to announce the availability of two reliable and proven Douglas aircraft for ACMI and Charter operations worldwide. DC9-34F (Freighter) – 1979 YOM: Max Payload: 15,000 kg, Total Volume: 85 CBM, Max Weight per Piece: 4,000 kg. DC9-31 (Passenger) – 1981 YOM: Configuration 100Y, Seating: 100–110 passengers. Both aircraft available for short-term and long-term ACMI agreements, ad-hoc charter.

Stranded alongside me in Doha based on fleet/ADS-B data. DOH: Air India Express B737-800, Gulf Air A320-200, Kuwait Airways A320-200N, Qatar Airways fleet details, Oman Air, Qatar Executive, RwandAir, SAUDI AIRLINES and many more aircraft currently at Doha. That is why the insurance sector calls it ground accumulation risk.

Breaking: Fire Alarm Activation at Dubai International Airport Following Drone Impact. Passengers and airport staff were seen evacuating sections of Dubai International Airport after fire alarms were triggered amid reports of a drone impact during a broader regional escalation involving Iran. EASA has issued advisory instructing airlines to not operate within affected airspace of Iran, Iraq, Israel, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia.

Important Travel Update: Due to regional airspace closures, US-Bangla Airlines has temporarily suspended flights to Muscat (MCT), Abu Dhabi (AUH), Dubai (DXB), Sharjah (SHJ), Doha (DOH), and Jeddah (JED) until further notice. Passengers are requested to check flight status on official website.
